variants.rewritten_question_text: At which value of x is f continuous but not differentiable?

variants.title: SCEMQA 开放题
variants.rewritten_question_text: The table shows values of $f'$, the derivative of function $f$. Although $f'$ is continuous over all real numbers, only selected values of $f'$ are shown. If $f'$ has exactly two real zeros, then $f$ is increasing over which of the following intervals?

rationale: Converted the multiple-choice prompt into a direct open-ended question asking for the x-value(s) explicitly.

variants.title: SCEMQA 开放题
variants.rewritten_question_text: Given that the second derivative of f is f''(x) = (x - a)^2(x - d), at which labeled value(s) of x does the graph of f have an inflection point?

rationale: The stem already directly asks for the value of f(1), so it can be kept as an open-ended question with the choices removed.

variants.rewritten_question_text: Shown in the diagram is a graph of $f'$, the derivative of function $f$. If $f(3) = 3$, then $f(1)=$?

rationale: The question requires interpreting a graph of f' and selecting the true statement. It is not a pure graph-label selection item, so it is kept and rewritten as a blank-style open question targeting the correct concept without options.
